DISCOVERY 301, DEFENDANTS ELITIGATION SOLUTIONS, INCORPORATED AND BRENT KINZEL'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS AT DEPOSITION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MUST ANSWER THE FIVE SPECIFIC, MINIMALLY INTRUSIVE QUESTIONS THAT SHE REFUSED TO ANSWER AT HER DEPOSITION. NO SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED GIVEN THE INADEQUACY OF DEFENDANT'S EFFORTS TO MEET AND CONFER. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED. = (301/PJB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers At Deposition
Off Calendar Nov-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANTS ELITIGATION SOLUTIONS, INCORPORATED and BRENT KINZEL'S Motion To Compel Answers At Deposition IS GRANTED. Plaintiff must answer the five specific, minimually intrusive questions that she refused to answer at her deposition. No sanctions are awarded given the inadequacy of defendant's efforts to meet and confer. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported. = (301/PJB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-18-2010 Set For Trial On Jul-11-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jun-24-11 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 613
